title: Session 68: Integral of sin^n(x) cos^m(x), Odd Exponents
content:
« Previous | Next »
Overview Finding antiderivatives of trigonometric functions can be tricky. In this session, guest lecturer Haynes Miller reviews some trigonometric identities and uses them to get antiderivatives for some products of sine and cosine.
Lecture Video and Notes Video Excerpts Clip 1: Review of Trigonometric Identities
Clip 2: Integral of sinn(x) cos(x)
Clip 3: Integral of sin3(x) cos2(x)
Clip 4: Integral of sin3(x)
